hero.subheading: A high-intent product launch engineered for speed, scarcity, and cultural pull.

background.title: High-Intent Funnel Design
description: Signet Sunday needed to convert brand interest into immediate revenue without relying on long-term nurture. Ideom designed a launch system focused on urgency, positioning, and controlled distribution to maximize conversion velocity.
background.quote: Ideom didn't just help us sell out — they engineered demand. The entire drop felt inevitable.

challenges.title: Challenges
description: Limited inventory required precise demand orchestration.
Need to create urgency without eroding brand perception.

solutions.title: Solutions
description: Reframed the drop as a limited cultural moment rather than a product release, increasing perceived value and urgency.
Built a streamlined path from awareness to checkout, minimizing friction and maximizing conversion speed.
Coordinated launch timing across channels to concentrate demand into a short window.
Developed a minimal, design-forward visual system that reinforced premium positioning and exclusivity.

emailSequence.title: We broke nearly every rule
description: 1. We sent to the entire list every single time, not just engaged segment (don't worry, we were monitoring deliverability the entire time. Remember, these were all extremely new subscribers).
2. We didn't follow typical email design rules
3. We didn't follow typical copywriting structures
We just sent emails that were on brand and entertaining. That's all people care about. Being entertained.

thoughtProcess.title: The thought process
content: We had finite time, wanted to maintain excitement, and didn't want to annoy our subscribers.
Miles needed to ensure he continued to produce quality social media content, and funnel people onto the list.
• the first two emails were entertaining and on brand
• the next two hyped up the launch date and ensured people were ready
• and that deliverability was on POINT, so that everyone would receive the launch email as intended
We also needed to ensure that the launch email was extremely simple.
For these, you just want to make sure that people know where to click.
Don't overdo your launch email. Keep it simple.

emails.label: Email 1
explanation: This actually took a long time to put together.
Everything needed to emulate Miles' social media content, to align with his brand identity.
Witty, satirical and funny.
Since everyone was new to the list, they were incentivised to open the email for information on the new products.
Then, we provided them with humorous content. A good trade and one that will have them coming back.
We sent this email in 3 batches (as it was our first email) to ensure we weren't burning our new domain.

emails.label: Email 2
explanation: We had some delays between email 1 & 2 due to some issues with stock.
We weren't sure when it was going to arrive after some manufacturing issues.
So we decided to not follow up with any emails until we had a date locked in.
This allowed email 2 to be;
Funny/Satirical
Provide an actual date
We knew we would get high engagement after the first email, so we didn't want to waste this by providing no tangible progress/updates on the shipment.
The results speak for themselves!

emails.label: Email 3
explanation: It was clear by now that;
our domain was healthy and we were landing in the inbox
and that majority of the list were going to open our next email regardless of what the subject line is
People were bought in, and they just wanted to hear more.
Usually, you'd opt to send 3x per week as a brand.
But the suspense and sporadic sending in a circumstance like this actually works in your favour.
Each and every email was going to be a banger, we had nothing to sell in the meantime, so there was no point overdoing it.
This email was purely used to drive in the date of the launch, and featured a countdown timer.
Slightly lower open rates here, but still great nonetheless.

emails.label: Email 4
explanation: This was the "24 hours to go" email.
All we wanted to do was;
remind them of the time
provide size chart so people could prepare
provide shipping locations
finish with something funny to stay on brand

emails.label: Email 5
explanation: The most simple of them all.
Provide the password for early access, then get out of there.
Just shooting for clicks.
Over 9,000 clicks to the site…

results.label: Inventory Sell-Through
results.desc: All units sold within the launch window

testimonials.quote: Ideom didn’t just help us sell out — they engineered demand. The entire drop felt inevitable.
